groups.label: aquarium
groups.description: Glass tank or aquarium containing fish, marine life, coral reefs

sentences.text: The marine biologist carefully monitored the temperature inside the tank to protect the tropical fish.
Bright neon tetras darted between the plastic plants anchored along the bottom of the tank.
She spent the afternoon rearranging the gravel and driftwood decorations inside her freshwater tank.
The children pressed their faces against the glass of the enormous shark tank at the aquarium.
He gently lowered the new angelfish into the tank after acclimating it in a plastic bag.
Every weekend the family gathered to watch the clownfish swim circles in their living room tank.
The pet store employee scooped a goldfish from the display tank and placed it in a bag.
A sea turtle glided past the viewing window of the massive rehabilitation tank at the center.
She noticed a thin crack forming along the bottom edge of the old glass tank on the shelf.
The hobbyist tested the ammonia levels in the tank every morning before leaving for work.
Colorful guppies chased each other through the bubbling water of the brightly lit tank.
He carefully scraped the algae off the inner walls of the tank with a magnetic cleaning tool.
The veterinarian examined the sick betta fish after removing it from the heated tank in the clinic.
Two hermit crabs crawled slowly across the sandy floor of the saltwater tank near the window.
The aquarist introduced live coral fragments into the reef tank during the scheduled maintenance session.
A small octopus squeezed through a gap in the rocks inside the research tank and startled the intern.
She poured the dechlorinated water slowly into the tank so the current would not disturb the fry.
The large tank in the lobby of the office building was home to a dozen colorful discus.
Visitors watched in awe as the manta ray swept gracefully through the curved observation tank.
The filtration system hummed quietly behind the tank while the fish settled in for the night.
A twenty-gallon tank with a hinged glass lid sat on the oak stand in the corner of the bedroom.
The reef tank glowed under the blue LED strip lights that simulated a deep ocean environment.
Her freshwater planted tank featured lush green carpets of dwarf hairgrass and floating frogbit.
The cylindrical tank in the center of the restaurant contained jellyfish pulsing with bioluminescent light.
An elegant saltwater tank with live rock and anemones served as the focal point of the waiting room.
The hospital's therapy wing had a large tank filled with slow-moving koi that calmed anxious patients.
Three layers of substrate lined the bottom of the planted tank to encourage healthy root growth.
The quarantine tank in the back room kept newly purchased fish isolated for two weeks.
A pair of seahorses clung to the artificial seagrass inside the tall narrow tank by the entrance.
The breeding tank was divided into three sections by transparent acrylic partitions to separate the fry.
A canister filter rated for fifty gallons provides excellent water clarity for any community tank.
Maintaining stable pH between 6.5 and 7.5 is essential for a healthy freshwater tropical tank.
Protein skimmers remove dissolved organic compounds before they accumulate in the reef tank water column.
The nitrogen cycle must be fully established before introducing livestock into a newly set-up tank.
Calcium reactors help maintain alkalinity and calcium levels required by stony corals in a reef tank.
Weekly water changes of ten to fifteen percent keep nitrate concentrations low in any planted tank.
Using a refractometer to measure salinity ensures that the specific gravity in the tank remains consistent.
Beneficial bacteria colonize the biological media inside the filter and stabilize the tank nitrogen cycle.
CO2 injection at two bubbles per second promotes vigorous plant growth in a high-tech planted tank.
LED fixtures with adjustable spectrum settings allow hobbyists to simulate sunrise and sunset over the tank.
The recommended stocking density for African cichlids is one inch of fish per two gallons of tank volume.
Activated carbon should be replaced monthly to maintain effective chemical filtration in any marine tank.
Copper-based medications must never be used in a tank that contains invertebrates or live coral.
A sump beneath the display tank houses the heater, skimmer, and return pump out of sight.
Drip acclimation over thirty minutes helps new arrivals adjust to the water parameters in the destination tank.
Powerheads positioned at opposite ends of the tank create circulation that mimics natural reef currents.
An automatic top-off system replenishes evaporated water and keeps the salinity stable in the reef tank.
I just set up my first tank and the little neon tetras are already schooling together beautifully.
My kid accidentally dropped a toy car into the tank and scared all the fish into hiding.
Dude, you should see the tank my neighbor built in his garage, it is absolutely massive.
I spent way too much money on new plants for the tank but they look so good.
The cat has been sitting on top of the tank lid all morning watching the fish swim.
We got this awesome little shrimp colony going in the tank and they keep multiplying like crazy.
My roommate forgot to plug the heater back in and the tank water got really cold overnight.
I found the perfect spot for the tank right next to the bookshelf in the living room.
Not gonna lie, cleaning the tank every week is kind of annoying but the fish deserve it.
Someone on the forum said I should add more hiding spots to my tank for the shy ones.
I swear this betta fish recognizes me every time I walk up to the tank to feed him.
My uncle has a giant tank in his basement that takes up nearly an entire wall.
The snail in the tank is the hardest worker in the house and keeps the glass spotless.
We had a power outage last night and I was so worried about the temperature in the tank.
I am thinking about upgrading to a bigger tank because these guys need more swimming room.
My daughter named every single fish in the tank and gets upset if we mix them up.
The bubbler in the tank makes a soothing noise that actually helps me fall asleep at night.
Our tank sprang a leak last Tuesday and we had to rush everything into a bucket.
The new LED light on the tank makes the colors of the fish pop way more than before.
Every time we have guests over, they spend at least ten minutes staring into the tank.
She positioned the thermometer strip on the outside of the tank where it was easy to read each morning.
The museum curator explained that the touch tank allowed visitors to handle starfish and sea urchins safely.
Water trickled softly from the overflow box at the back of the tank into the sump below.
The school installed a small tank in each classroom so students could observe aquatic ecosystems firsthand.
A bristlenose pleco attached itself to the glass near the top of the tank and refused to move.
He ordered a custom-built rimless tank with low-iron glass to maximize the clarity of the display.
The dentist's office featured a tall hexagonal tank filled with exotic lionfish and pufferfish.
After months of planning, she finally filled the tank with water and began the cycling process.
Tiny fry emerged from the cave where the cichlid parents guarded their nest inside the tank.
The hotel lobby displayed a floor-to-ceiling tank stocked with graceful moon jellyfish and soft blue lighting.
He fashioned a DIY tank divider from craft mesh so the aggressive fish could not reach the others.
A UV sterilizer inline with the return pump helps control harmful pathogens circulating through the tank.
Dosing trace elements like iodine and strontium supports invertebrate health in a mature reef tank.
Maintaining a photoperiod of eight to ten hours prevents excessive algae blooms in a planted tank.
Cycling a new tank using pure ammonia typically takes four to six weeks to complete.
Reverse osmosis water eliminates chloramine and heavy metals before it enters the tank during water changes.
I honestly forgot I even had a tank until I heard the filter making a weird grinding noise.
My friend wants to put a lobster in his tank and I told him that sounds like trouble.
The water in the tank turned green almost overnight so I did an emergency water change.
I really want a saltwater tank but everyone says it is way harder to maintain than freshwater.
Honestly, the most relaxing part of my day is sitting on the couch watching the tank.
The predatory tank at the exhibition housed piranhas behind thick reinforced glass panels for safety.
A wave maker mounted to the side of the tank simulated tidal movement for the corals.
Flow rate through the sump should equal five to ten times the total tank volume per hour.
She knelt beside the tank and watched a crayfish slowly emerge from its hiding spot under the log.
The old man tapped gently on the glass of the tank and the pufferfish swam closer to investigate.
The volunteer cleaned the observation tank at the nature center while explaining each species to the kids.
My mom got me a tank for my birthday and I already have five different species picked out.
The corals in the nano tank fluoresced brilliantly under the actinic blue lights at night.
Substrate depth of two inches is generally sufficient for most rooted plants in a standard tank.
That tank in the corner of the cafe always catches my eye when I walk past.
The eel slithered through a narrow PVC pipe wedged between rocks at the bottom of the tank.
The automatic feeder dispensed a pinch of flake food into the tank at precisely eight each morning.

groups.label: vehicle
groups.description: Military tank, armored vehicle, battle tank contexts

sentences.text: The commander ordered the tank to advance across the muddy terrain toward the enemy position.
A lone tank rolled through the devastated city streets while soldiers moved cautiously behind it.
The crew scrambled into the tank and sealed the hatch before the mortar barrage began.
An enemy rocket struck the side armor of the tank but failed to penetrate the hull.
The general watched through binoculars as the lead tank crested the ridge and opened fire.
Three soldiers crouched behind the disabled tank using its massive chassis as cover from incoming rounds.
The driver steered the tank through a narrow gap between two collapsed buildings without scraping the walls.
A column of smoke rose from the burning tank that had been hit by an anti-armor missile.
The sergeant radioed headquarters to report that the tank had thrown a track in the soft mud.
Under heavy rain, the tank churned through the flooded field, spraying water in all directions.
Engineers worked through the night to repair the damaged turret ring on the disabled tank.
The tank fired its main gun and the deafening blast echoed across the entire valley.
Infantry soldiers used the tank as a mobile shield while they crossed the open ground.
The historian described how a single tank had held the bridge for six hours during the siege.
Mechanics loaded spare parts onto a flatbed truck destined for the damaged tank at the forward base.
A decommissioned tank stood on a concrete pedestal outside the military museum as a memorial.
The scout spotted an enemy tank concealed beneath camouflage netting at the edge of the forest.
Rust covered the hull of the abandoned tank that had sat in the desert for decades.
The lieutenant climbed out of the tank and surveyed the landscape through a pair of field glasses.
During the parade, a vintage tank rumbled down the main boulevard to cheers from the crowd.
The documentary featured rare footage of the first tank ever used in combat during World War One.
A heavy transport aircraft carried the tank across the ocean to the staging area overseas.
The turret of the tank rotated smoothly as the gunner tracked a target moving along the tree line.
Camouflage paint in a desert pattern covered every surface of the tank to reduce its visibility.
The tank commander gave the order to halt and the massive vehicle groaned to a stop.
Battle scars and dents across the front plate of the tank told the story of many engagements.
A recovery vehicle towed the immobilized tank back to the repair depot behind the front lines.
The children at the air show climbed onto the parked tank and pretended to be soldiers.
Sandbags and razor wire surrounded the tank that served as a checkpoint along the main road.
The veteran described the deafening noise inside the tank during a live-fire engagement.
Composite armor arrays on modern main battle platforms give the tank superior protection against shaped charges.
The tank utilizes a 120mm smoothbore cannon capable of firing both kinetic and chemical energy rounds.
Reactive armor tiles mounted on the tank hull detonate outward to neutralize incoming anti-armor projectiles.
A gas turbine engine powers the tank and provides a top speed of roughly forty-five miles per hour.
The fire control system allows the tank to engage targets accurately while moving at full speed.
Thermal imaging sights on the tank enable the crew to identify targets through smoke and darkness.
The tank carries forty rounds of main gun ammunition stored in a blast-proof compartment behind the turret.
An auxiliary power unit allows the tank to operate its electronics without running the main engine.
Active protection systems intercept incoming projectiles milliseconds before they reach the tank hull.
Track tension must be inspected after every fifty miles of operation to prevent the tank from shedding links.
The depleted uranium armor package on the latest variant gives the tank unmatched frontal protection.
Ammunition is loaded into the tank using an autoloader mechanism that eliminates the need for a human loader.
A laser rangefinder integrated with the ballistic computer ensures the tank achieves first-round hit probability above ninety percent.
The nuclear, biological, and chemical filtration system keeps the crew safe inside the sealed tank during contamination events.
Suspension upgrades allow the tank to traverse rough terrain at higher speeds without excessive crew fatigue.
Infrared signature reduction coatings make the tank harder to detect with heat-seeking sensors at night.
Battlefield data links allow the tank to share targeting information in real time with other armored units.
That tank at the museum is insanely huge and I could not believe people actually fit inside it.
My grandpa drove a tank in the war and still talks about it every Thanksgiving dinner.
We watched a video of a tank crushing a row of old cars and it was wild.
There is a place in Nevada where you can actually pay to drive a real tank around.
My buddy has a model tank collection in his basement with at least fifty different ones.
I saw a tank driving down the highway on a flatbed truck on my way to work today.
The game lets you customize your tank with different armor, weapons, and paint schemes before each battle.
Imagine having a tank parked in your driveway, the neighbors would absolutely lose their minds.
Somebody posted a video of a tank getting stuck in a ditch and needing another one to pull it out.
My little brother only wants tank toys for his birthday and our house is full of them.
That old tank in the park has been there since before I was born and kids climb on it daily.
We found a documentary about a tank crew that survived three direct hits during the desert campaign.
My friend says being inside a tank is incredibly loud and cramped with barely any room to move.
I would never want to stand in front of a tank because even the ground shakes when it moves.
The guys at the reenactment brought a working tank and drove it across the field for everyone to watch.
Someone on social media asked whether a tank or a bulldozer would win in a pushing contest.
That new war movie has an incredible scene where a tank plows through a concrete barricade like nothing.
Villagers stood along the roadside and watched the tank convoy pass through their town at dawn.
The photographer captured a striking image of a lone tank silhouetted against the orange desert sunset.
An improvised explosive device detonated beneath the lead tank but the reinforced hull absorbed most of the blast.
The old farmer found a rusted tank half-buried in his field, a relic from the battle decades ago.
Dust clouds trailed behind the tank as it barreled across the open steppe at full throttle.
The radio operator inside the tank relayed coordinates to the artillery battery positioned behind the hills.
Modular armor kits allow field crews to reconfigure the tank protection level depending on the threat environment.
Multi-spectral camouflage netting reduces the visual and thermal signature of the tank during static defense operations.
The hydraulic turret drive enables the tank to slew its main gun three hundred sixty degrees in under nine seconds.
Have you ever seen a tank fire at night because the muzzle flash lights up everything around it?
I played a VR game where you sit inside a tank and it honestly felt claustrophobic.
During the liberation, the townspeople draped flowers and flags over the hull of the first allied tank to arrive.
The platoon leader directed the tank into a concealed position behind the stone wall overlooking the crossroads.
He sketched the outline of the destroyed tank in his field journal while waiting for the convoy.
The loader slammed a fresh round into the breech as the tank lurched forward over the rubble.
Automatic track tensioning systems reduce maintenance downtime and keep the tank operational during extended deployments.
I built a scale model tank over the weekend and it turned out way better than I expected.
A crater in the road forced the tank to detour through the adjacent wheat field toward the bridge.
The war correspondent rode on top of the tank as it rolled into the newly liberated city.
Smoke grenade launchers mounted on the turret allow the tank to create an instant concealment screen.
My cousin showed me around the military base and we got to sit inside a real tank.
The memorial plaque beside the tank listed the names of all the crew members who served in it.
The tank weighed over sixty tons yet still managed to cross the temporary pontoon bridge intact.
Urban warfare doctrine recommends that infantry always accompany the tank to guard against close-range threats.
I wonder what it is like to be a tank driver because the visibility looks pretty terrible from inside.
The wreath ceremony took place beside the restored tank that had served in the famous winter offensive.
The prototype tank incorporated a hybrid electric drive system to reduce noise and fuel consumption on patrol.
He painted a small flag on the side of the model tank and placed it on the display shelf.
The reconnaissance drone transmitted live footage of the enemy tank position to the brigade command post.
When the tank emerged from the tree line, the defenders realized their barricade would not hold.
The museum guide explained that this particular tank had seen action on three different continents.
Drone footage showed the enemy tank reversing into a building for concealment as the strike aircraft approached.
The logistics officer calculated how many rail cars would be needed to transport every tank in the brigade.
A direct hit from the anti-armor round disabled the tank and forced the crew to evacuate on foot.
Satellite imagery revealed a freshly dug revetment designed to conceal one tank from aerial observation.
The kids at the history fair built an impressive cardboard replica of a famous World War Two tank.

groups.label: scuba
groups.description: Diving tank, oxygen tank, compressed air tank contexts

sentences.text: The dive instructor checked the pressure gauge on the tank before they entered the water.
She strapped the heavy tank onto her back and waddled to the edge of the boat.
The regulator hissed softly as he drew his first breath from the freshly filled tank.
They loaded six aluminum dive cylinders and one steel tank onto the charter boat for the morning trip.
His tank ran low at sixty feet and the dive computer warned him to begin his ascent.
The divemaster inspected each tank for dents and corrosion before clearing them for the afternoon dive.
A steady stream of bubbles rose from the tank as the diver descended into the blue abyss.
She tightened the valve on the tank and handed it over the side of the dock to her partner.
The compressor room at the dive shop was filled with rows of freshly filled cylinders and one spare tank.
He rinsed the salt residue off the tank with fresh water after every ocean dive.
The cave diver mounted a sidemount tank on each hip for better streamlining in narrow passages.
After surfacing, the group handed their gear up to the boat crew, starting with the heavy tank.
The rescue swimmer grabbed a spare tank from the helicopter basket and jumped into the rough sea.
The young diver nervously checked the tank valve three times before stepping off the platform.
Coral formations towered above them as bubbles from the tank spiraled upward toward the sunlit surface.
She felt the weight of the tank shift as the current pushed her sideways along the reef wall.
The guide signaled for everyone to check their tank pressure and report their remaining air supply.
A steel 80-cubic-foot tank provides approximately one hour of bottom time at shallow recreational depths.
Visual inspection of the tank interior must be performed annually to check for pitting and corrosion.
Hydrostatic testing every five years verifies that the tank can safely withstand its rated working pressure.
Nitrox-compatible tank valves must be cleaned with oxygen-safe lubricants to prevent combustion risks.
The burst disk on the tank valve acts as a safety release if internal pressure exceeds the limit.
Aluminum tanks are lighter on land but negatively buoyant when empty, unlike a steel tank which stays negative throughout.
Fill pressure for a standard recreational tank is typically between 2500 and 3000 pounds per square inch.
A DIN valve connection provides a more secure seal than a yoke fitting on a high-pressure tank.
Enriched air Nitrox at 32 percent oxygen extends no-decompression limits when breathed from a properly labeled tank.
The O-ring seated between the regulator first stage and the tank valve must be inspected before every dive.
Technical divers often carry a stage tank clipped to D-rings on their harness for decompression gas.
Gas blending for trimix requires filling the tank with precise ratios of helium, oxygen, and nitrogen.
The standing pressure in a tank drops as water temperature decreases, which divers must account for when planning.
Marking a tank with the MOD depth and oxygen percentage is mandatory before every Nitrox dive.
A manifold connects twin back-mounted cylinders into a single gas supply while still allowing the diver to isolate each tank.
Surface air consumption rate helps predict how long a diver's tank will last at any given depth.
The compressor's moisture separator must function properly to ensure that only dry air enters the tank.
I almost dropped the tank getting out of the boat and my dive buddy gave me such a look.
How do you even carry that tank around because it looks like it weighs a ton.
My first time scuba diving I forgot to open the tank valve and could not figure out why I could not breathe.
The rental gear at the resort was fine but the tank felt super heavy on my back.
My instructor said I go through air way too fast and should get a bigger tank next time.
We got back on the boat and I could not wait to get that tank off my shoulders.
I bought my own tank because renting one every weekend was getting ridiculously expensive.
Someone accidentally knocked my tank off the bench and it made the loudest clang I have ever heard.
The dive shop guy said my tank needs to be retested before they will fill it again.
I was so nervous before my first dive that I kept checking my tank pressure every thirty seconds.
Honestly, the hardest part of diving is getting the tank on while the boat is rocking back and forth.
My buddy and I share a locker at the dive club where we store our gear and a spare tank.
I signed up for a wreck dive next month and need to get my tank serviced before then.
The boat captain stacked the refilled cylinders in the rack and secured each tank with a bungee cord.
She descended slowly along the anchor line, checking her tank gauge at every ten-foot interval.
The underwater photographer balanced the camera housing in one hand while adjusting the tank strap with the other.
At the safety stop, he watched his remaining air dwindle as the tank gauge crept toward the red zone.
They emerged from the cenote and carried each tank up the limestone steps to the waiting van.
The night dive began with each diver clicking on a glow stick attached to the top of their tank.
Warm tropical water surrounded them as they kicked gently along the reef, bubbles trailing from each tank.
The volunteer search team distributed a fresh tank to every diver before the recovery operation resumed.
He sat on the gunwale, placed the regulator in his mouth, and rolled backward with the tank into the sea.
The deep wreck dive required each team member to carry a primary and a bailout tank.
She surfaced with a smile, gave the okay signal, and passed her empty tank up to the crew.
Salt crystals formed around the valve opening of the tank that had been left drying in the sun.
Continuous flow regulators deliver gas from the tank at a constant rate regardless of ambient pressure.
Galvanic corrosion can occur where the aluminum tank contacts dissimilar metals on the backplate.
My sister tried scuba once, hated carrying the tank on her back, and stuck with snorkeling after that.
After the dive, we all sat on the dock comparing how much air was left in each tank.
I keep forgetting which way to turn the valve on the tank and always have to ask.
The guide told us to stay close together and always keep our buddy's tank in sight.
He handed the filled tank across the counter with both hands because of its considerable weight.
The marine park required all divers to show proof of tank inspection before entering the protected reef.
Twin cylinders joined by a manifold provide redundancy in case one tank valve malfunctions during a deep dive.
Maximum operating depth for a tank filled with 36 percent Nitrox is approximately ninety-five feet.
I cannot believe how fast I burned through that tank on my first deep dive.
Do you think it is worth buying your own tank or should I just keep renting from the shop?
The freediving coach explained that learning breath-hold techniques could reduce a diver's tank consumption dramatically.
A rust-colored ring inside the neck of the tank indicated that a visual inspection was overdue.
Every diver clipped a small strobe light to the bottom of their tank for the night dive.
Tank boots made of rubber protect the rounded bottom of the cylinder and allow it to stand upright.
My dive log says I had 800 PSI left in the tank when I surfaced from the second dive.
She felt the reassuring weight of the full tank settle onto her BCD as she geared up on the boat.
The rebreather recirculates exhaled gas instead of venting it, drastically extending the duration of each tank.
I always get a little nervous when the needle on the tank gauge hits the halfway mark.
Eddy current testing can detect micro-cracks in the neck threads of an aluminum tank before they become dangerous.
The diver clipped the stage tank to her left hip and descended toward the first decompression stop.
We had to cancel the dive because the shop could not get the compressor running to fill our tank.
The expedition leader calculated that twelve full cylinders plus one emergency tank would supply the team for three days.
Overexpansion of gas in the lungs can occur if a diver ascends too fast while breathing from the tank.
I scratched my name into the tape on my tank so nobody would grab it by mistake at the shop.
The island's only dive operator stored more than a hundred cylinders in a shaded rack behind each tank filling station.
Breathing resistance increases as the pressure inside the tank decreases during a dive at depth.
The new student practiced donning and doffing the tank in the shallow end of the pool.
Everybody at the resort was jealous when I showed up with my own custom-painted tank.
A slow leak from the O-ring caused tiny bubbles to stream from the top of her tank throughout the dive.
Carbon monoxide contamination in a breathing gas tank can cause unconsciousness and is potentially fatal underwater.
That rental tank was scratched up and ugly but it worked perfectly fine for the entire dive.
The marine biologist secured a sampling kit to the harness alongside the tank before entering the kelp forest.
Color codes painted on the shoulder of the tank indicate the type of breathing gas mixture inside.
That was the fastest I have ever gone through a tank and I think the cold water was the reason.
The advanced open water course included a deep dive where students learned to manage tank air at depth.
Positive pressure in the first stage prevents water from entering the tank valve during submersion.
She hauled the rented tank across the beach parking lot in a wheeled cart.
Balanced piston regulators deliver consistent breathing performance regardless of the remaining pressure inside the tank.
Honestly the worst part about traveling for diving is figuring out where to get a tank filled.

groups.label: septic
groups.description: Septic tank, sewage tank, water storage tank, fuel tank contexts

sentences.text: The plumber discovered that the underground tank had developed a small crack and was leaking into the soil.
Raw sewage backed up into the yard after the septic tank overflowed during the heavy rainstorm.
The inspector lowered a camera into the tank to assess the condition of the interior walls.
A pumping truck arrived at dawn to empty the full septic tank before the ground froze.
The homeowner hired a contractor to replace the corroded steel tank with a new concrete one.
Foul odors drifted across the lawn because the tank had not been pumped in over five years.
Workers dug a trench from the house to the new tank location at the far end of the property.
The real estate agent warned buyers that the septic tank would need replacement within the next two years.
He marked the location of the buried tank with a metal stake so the pump truck could find it.
A tree root had grown through the wall of the tank and caused a persistent blockage in the drain field.
The farmer filled the large water storage tank from the well every morning to supply the livestock.
Diesel fuel seeped from the cracked underground tank and contaminated the groundwater near the gas station.
A cylindrical steel tank behind the factory stored thousands of gallons of industrial solvent.
The heating oil delivery driver connected a hose to the fill pipe on the basement tank.
Rainwater flowed off the metal roof and into the collection tank positioned beneath the downspout.
The propane tank beside the grill was nearly empty so he drove to the hardware store for a refill.
An enormous stainless steel tank at the brewery held the fermenting beer at a precisely controlled temperature.
The village relied on a single concrete tank at the top of the hill for its entire water supply.
She noticed a rust stain on the ground beneath the outdoor heating oil tank and called the service company.
The construction crew lowered the precast concrete tank into the excavated pit using a crane.
Gravity carries effluent from the house through a four-inch pipe into the first compartment of the septic tank.
Anaerobic bacteria inside the tank break down organic solids into sludge, scum, and clarified effluent.
A baffle wall inside the tank prevents floating scum from passing directly into the outlet pipe.
The tank should be pumped every three to five years depending on household size and water usage.
Secondary containment around an above-ground fuel tank must hold at least 110 percent of the vessel capacity.
Cathodic protection systems slow corrosion on buried steel components of the tank and extend its service life.
Fiberglass-reinforced polyester is a common material for underground storage because the tank resists both corrosion and crushing.
A float switch inside the tank activates the alarm when the liquid level rises above the safe threshold.
Leak detection sensors placed beneath the tank floor trigger an alert if fuel migrates beyond the containment area.
Double-wall construction ensures that any breach in the inner shell is captured before liquid escapes the tank.
The local code requires that the drain field be located at least ten feet from the edge of the tank.
Effluent filters installed on the outlet tee of the tank reduce suspended solids entering the drain field.
Soil percolation tests determine whether the ground can absorb the effluent discharged from the tank at an adequate rate.
A riser extends the tank access lid to grade level so the system can be inspected without excavation.
Venting the tank through a roof stack prevents dangerous gas buildup and allows proper drainage flow.
Ultra-low sulfur diesel stored in the tank requires additives to prevent microbial growth inside the vessel.
A polyethylene tank is lightweight and resistant to most chemicals, making it ideal for rainwater storage applications.
Our septic tank backed up right before the holiday and the whole house smelled absolutely terrible.
The guy who pumped the tank said it was the worst one he had seen in twenty years.
I had no idea the tank was under the driveway until the inspector pointed it out on the map.
My neighbor ran over the tank lid with his riding mower and cracked it right in half.
We just moved to the country and apparently we have to get the tank pumped every few years.
The propane tank ran out in the middle of grilling and we had to finish the burgers inside.
I forgot to check the oil tank gauge and we ran out of heat on the coldest night of the year.
The water tank on the roof of the cabin needs to be filled before we can take showers.
Dad spent the whole weekend trying to find the buried tank with a metal detector and a lot of patience.
The landlord says the tank is fine but I keep smelling something weird in the backyard.
Getting the tank replaced cost us almost five thousand dollars and that was the cheapest estimate we got.
I really do not want to think about what is inside that tank but somebody has to deal with it.
The gas station attendant mentioned that the underground fuel tank was just replaced last summer.
Somebody on our street got a huge fine because their tank was leaking into the storm drain.
We collect rainwater in a big plastic tank behind the garage and use it to water the garden.
That rusty old tank behind the shed has been there since my grandparents owned this property.
My brother-in-law tried to install the tank himself and ended up flooding the entire backyard.
The environmental agency ordered the gas station to excavate and replace the leaking underground storage tank immediately.
Thick mud clung to the excavator bucket as the operator carefully unearthed the old concrete tank.
A large stainless steel tank gleamed in the afternoon sun on the winery's production floor.
The firefighter connected a hose to the portable water tank and began dousing the smoldering brush.
Floodwaters lifted the buried plastic tank out of the ground and deposited it sideways in the yard.
The fuel delivery truck backed up to the fill port and transferred three hundred gallons into the tank.
A gauge on the side of the propane tank showed that only a quarter of the supply remained.
Workers welded a patch over the corroded section of the industrial tank to stop the chemical leak.
The rainwater harvesting system routed all gutter runoff through a first-flush diverter before entering the storage tank.
She measured the fuel level in the basement tank with a dipstick and wrote the reading in her logbook.
The abandoned factory had a row of rusted cylindrical chemical storage units and one intact tank near the loading dock.
He tapped the side of the metal tank with a wrench and listened for the hollow ring.
Annual inspection of the tank interstitial space is required to verify the integrity of the secondary containment.
Hydrostatic pressure testing confirms that the tank shell can sustain its rated capacity without deformation.
A submersible pump within the dosing tank delivers effluent to the drain field at controlled intervals.
Impressed current cathodic protection requires a rectifier and anode bed installed adjacent to the buried steel tank.
The maintenance schedule calls for draining and cleaning the sediment from the bottom of the tank every six months.
Honestly, dealing with the tank is the worst part of owning a house in a rural area.
